entity.name: AI Builders Foundation
entity.description: A 501(c)(3) public-charity nonprofit (EIN 42-1986706) building an open, vendor-neutral 'university for AI builders' — free curriculum, hackathon starter kits, and community tooling that teach anyone to build full-stack AI applications. Events, courses, community, and open source, given away for the public good.

projects.name: AI Builders Curriculum
projects.description: An open-source, vendor-neutral curriculum and set of hackathon starter kits for learning to build AI applications end-to-end — data, auth, functions, model/agent integration, and deployment. MIT-licensed teaching material used in the Foundation's free hackathons and cohorts, clonable by any educator or self-learner. The 'courses + events + open source' pillars of the Foundation, as code.

channels.address: AI Builders Foundation — details provided on request
channels.description: US nonprofit operating account (AI Builders Foundation, EIN 42-1986706).

plans.name: Seed the open curriculum + starter kits
plans.description: Author and open-source the first full AI-builder curriculum track (8-12 modules) plus 5 hackathon starter kits, all MIT-licensed. Covers technical writing, example apps, tests, and CI so the material is maintainable and clonable by any educator.

plans.name: Annual maintenance + hackathon runs
plans.description: Keep the curriculum current with a fast-moving field, run 3-4 free community hackathons per year against the starter kits, and support contributors. Sustains the open educational resource beyond the initial build.
